# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create Docker Compose configuration for ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana). Define services for log collection, storage, and visualization. Configure Logstash pipelines for log parsing. Expose Kibana UI and Elasticsearch ports for access. This is for our centralized logging platform.
# Category: elasticsearch_logs_exposed

version: '3.8'

services:
  elasticsearch:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.10.4
    container_name: elasticsearch
    environment:
      - ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xmx512m -Xms512m
      - xpack.security.enabled=false
      - discovery.type=single-node
    ulimits:
      memlock:
        soft: -1
        hard: -1
    volumes:
      - elasticsearch_data:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data
    ports:
      - "9200:9200"
      - "9300:9300"
    networks:
      - elk-network
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "curl -f http://localhost:9200 || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

  logstash:
    image: docker.elastic.co/logstash/logstash:8.10.4
    container_name: logstash
    environment:
      - LS_JAVA_OPTS=-Xmx512m -Xms512m
    volumes:
      - ./logstash/config/logstash.yml:/usr/share/logstash/config/logstash.yml:ro
      - ./logstash/pipeline:/usr/share/logstash/pipeline:ro
    ports:
      - "5044:5044" # Beats input
      - "5000:5000/tcp" # Custom TCP input for plain logs
      - "5000:5000/udp" # Custom UDP input for syslog
    networks:
      - elk-network
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "logstash-plugin", "list"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5

  kibana:
    image: docker.elastic.co/kibana/kibana:8.10.4
    container_name: kibana
    environment:
      - ELASTICSEARCH_HOSTS=http://elasticsearch:9200
    volumes:
      - ./kibana/config/kibana.yml:/usr/share/kibana/config/kibana.yml:ro
    ports:
      - "5601:5601"
    networks:
      - elk-network
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "curl -f http://localhost:5601/api/status || exit 1"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5

volumes:
  elasticsearch_data:
    driver: local

networks:
  elk-network:
    driver: bridge

# To run this compose file:
# 1. Create the following directory structure:
#    .
#    ├── docker-compose.yml
#    ├── logstash
#    │   ├── config
#    │   │   └── logstash.yml
#    │   └── pipeline
#    │       ├── 01-beats-input.conf
#    │       ├── 02-syslog-input.conf
#    │       └── 03-app-logs.conf
#    └── kibana
#        └── config
#            └── kibana.yml
#
# 2. Populate the configuration files:
#
#    logstash/config/logstash.yml:
#    # Example Logstash configuration
#    http.host: "0.0.0.0"
#    path.config: /usr/share/logstash/pipeline
#    xpack.monitoring.enabled: false
#
#    logstash/pipeline/01-beats-input.conf:
#    input {
#      beats {
#        port => 5044
#      }
#    }
#    output {
#      elasticsearch {
#        hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#        index => "beats-logs-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
#      }
#    }
#
#    logstash/pipeline/02-syslog-input.conf:
#    input {
#      udp {
#        port => 5000
#        type => "syslog"
#      }
#    }
#    filter {
#      if [type] == "syslog" {
#        grok {
#          match => { "message" => "<%{POSINT:syslog_pri}>%{SYSLOGTIMESTAMP:syslog_timestamp} %{SYSLOGHOST:syslog_hostname} %{PROG:syslog_program}(?:\[%{POSINT:syslog_pid}\])?: %{GREEDYDATA:syslog_message}" }
#          add_tag => ["syslog_parsed"]
#        }
#        date {
#          match => ["syslog_timestamp", "MMM  d HH:mm:ss", "MMM dd HH:mm:ss"]
#          target => "@timestamp"
#        }
#      }
#    }
#    output {
#      if [type] == "syslog" {
#        elasticsearch {
#          hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#          index => "syslog-logs-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
#        }
#      }
#    }
#
#    logstash/pipeline/03-app-logs.conf:
#    input {
#      tcp {
#        port => 5000
#        type => "app_logs"
#        codec => json # Assuming your application logs are JSON
#      }
#    }
#    filter {
#      if [type] == "app_logs" {
#        # Example: if JSON logs don't have a timestamp, add current
#        if ![timestamp] {
#          date {
#            target => "@timestamp"
#            force => true
#          }
#        }
#        # Example: Add an environment field for centralized logging
#        mutate {
#          add_field => { "environment" => "production" }
#        }
#      }
#    }
#    output {
#      if [type] == "app_logs" {
#        elasticsearch {
#          hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#          index => "application-logs-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
#        }
#      }
#    }
#
#    kibana/config/kibana.yml:
#    # Example Kibana configuration
#    server.host: "0.0.0.0"
#    elasticsearch.hosts: ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#    # Uncomment the following to disable X-Pack security if Elasticsearch has it disabled
#    # xpack.security.enabled: false
#
# 3. Run `docker compose up -d` in the directory containing `docker-compose.yml`.
